creator: jf@dockes.org
text:
Sunpoet Po-Chuan Hsieh writes:
 > On Fri, Oct 07, 2011 at 06:00:33PM +0000, Jean-Francois Dockes wrote:
 > >  There is already a patch in the database for an update to 1.2.3, it is
 > >  almost one year old.
 > >  
 > >  The migration of xapian to version 1.2 is waiting for a so-called repocopy
 > >  from xapian to xapian10 (the reason for wanting to keep xapian10 so long is
 > >  unknown to me).
 > >  
 > >  Henrik Brix Andersen <brix@FreeBSD.org> has as far as I know prepared a
 > >  patch to make the repocopy happen. Last I heard about it was june 2011.
 > >  
 > >  I already have a patch ready for an update to 1.2.7 (once the repository
 > >  situation is resolved), so the one in this PR is redundant, sorry.
 > >  
 > >  J.F. Dockes
 > 
 > Hi,
 > 
 > brix@ is cc'd.
 > 
 > What I know about the current status is:
 > 1) 4 PRs in the database for xapian 1.2.3 but no one takes them currently.
 > 2) Repocopy was abandoned.
 > 
 > That's why I submit these PRs for updating xapian ports to latest version.
 > Personally, I think it's OK to update xapian from 1.0.x to 1.2.x without
 > keeping the old versions. PORTREVISION bump is enough for those ports
 > depend on xapian ports. Regardless of the status of old PRs, if you agree
 > with the update (1.0.x to 1.2.7), we (brix, me or other committers) would
 > do the update right now.

Ok then, but there are two things that I've a problem with in the
xapian-core patch:

 - Defaulting to SSE2: (except if this is now official/mandatory FreeBSD
   policy). Last time I tried this, it happened that a few Xapian installs
   were getting illegal instruction signals, and I had to backpedal quite
   fast (I had been caught unaware by a Xapian change actually).

 - Except if I'm missing something, we need a depend for uuid stuff:
 LIB_DEPENDS=	uuid:${PORTSDIR}/misc/e2fsprogs-libuuid

More generally, I have had patches ready for 1.2.[4-7] more or less as soon as
they came out, but I was waiting for the repocopy situation to clear.

I could start again with your patches but, I already have 2 issues
with the first one I take a look at.

I wished you had contacted me before making this patches, but at this
points we have 2 possibilities which both suit me:

 - Either I just give up the maintainance of the xapian ports to you

 - Or I go on with maintaining these, but we should take my current version
   of the 1.2.7 ports, possibly including changes that you wish to make,
   for example the different approach to manage noportdocs.

Cheers,

jf
